title: Domestic Violence Hotlines * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Domestic Violence Hotlines * Native American Community 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Deafness LGBTQ2+ Issues Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS GOVERNMENT OFFICE CIRCLES OF HEALING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, information and referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 1245 Fulton Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, and human trafficking. Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 808-8152 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE UMATILLA INDIAN RESERVATION FAMILY VIOLENCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence, sexual assault, rape, stalking, teen dating violence, and elder abuse with safety planning and guidance through the justice systems (Tribal, state, and federal). 46411 Timine Way Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic or intimate partner violence, sexual assault, rape, stalking, teen dating violence, and elder abuse Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm Email (541) 240-4171 Get Directions Visit Website More Details STRONGHEARTS NATIVE HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers confidential and anonymous domestic and sexual violence helpline. Provides culturally-appropriate, peer-to-peer support as well as crisis intervention, assistance with safety planning, education, and referrals to Native-centered domestic and sexual violence service providers. PO Box 22293 Eagan, MN 55122 Eligibility: American Indian and Alaska Natives affected by domestic, dating, and sexual violence. Also serves affected family members, friends, service providers, and people who may question their abusive behavior. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (844) 762-8483 - 247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS CIRCLES OF HEALING CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE UMATILLA INDIAN RESERVATION FAMILY VIOLENCE SERVICES
